title: onobrychis arenaria subsp. sibirica
intro: Onobrychis arenaria subsp. sibirica, also known as Siberian bird's-foot trefoil, is a plant species within the pea family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of saponins and flavonoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Onobrychis arenaria subsp. sibirica is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
